sections.paragraph:
σύγγραμμα, ατος, τό (Hdt. et al.) someth. composed in writing, book, (written) work (X., Mem. 2, 1, 21; 4, 2, 10; Pla., Ep. 2, 314c; Philo, Vi. Cont. 29; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 44; 2, 288; Just.) EpilMosq 1, 2,4.—Of the Apology of Quadratus, Eus., HE 4, 3, 1. τοῦ δὲ θεοφίλου … τρία τὰ πρὸς Αὐτόλυκον … συγγράμματα 4, 24.—DELG s.v. γράφω.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

someth. someth. = something

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

EpilMosq EpilMosq = Epilogus Mosquensis—List 1

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 951
